highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has released Claude Opus 4.7, the latest addition to the Opus model family. Developers using Claude Code should check the release notes to understand new capabilities that may enhance their AI-assisted coding workflows in VS Code.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.136: New Auto Mode Hard-Deny Rules & OTEL Feedback Survey
highlights.summary: Version 2.1.136 adds `settings.autoMode.hard_deny` — classifier rules that unconditionally block certain actions regardless of user intent — giving enterprise teams stronger guardrails in automated workflows. It also re-enables the session quality survey for enterprises using OpenTelemetry via the `CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_FEEDBACK_SURVEY_FOR_OTEL` environment variable.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.133: Worktree Base Branch Control
highlights.summary: The new `worktree.baseRef` setting lets you choose whether `--worktree`, `EnterWorktree`, and agent-isolation worktrees branch from `origin/<default>` or your local `HEAD`. The default value `fresh` restores `EnterWorktree` to branching from the remote default, so existing users should review this change to avoid unexpected behavior.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.132: Session ID in Bash Tool & Alternate Screen Opt-Out
highlights.summary: Claude Code now injects `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` into every Bash tool subprocess, making it easy to correlate shell actions with a specific session in hooks or logging pipelines. You can also set `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_ALTERNATE_SCREEN=1` to keep the conversation output in your normal terminal scroll buffer instead of the fullscreen renderer.

highlights.title: Claude Code CVE-2026-39861: Sandbox Escape via Symlink
highlights.summary: A security advisory has been published for Claude Code describing a sandbox escape vulnerability triggered via symlink manipulation. Developers running Claude Code in automated or shared environments should update to the latest version immediately and review their file-system permissions.

highlights.title: Using Claude Code: The Unreasonable Effectiveness of HTML
highlights.summary: This piece explores how Claude Code excels at generating plain HTML, often producing surprisingly complete and functional results with minimal prompting. If you are new to AI-assisted development, starting with simple HTML projects is a great low-risk way to build confidence with Claude Code before moving to complex frameworks.

highlights.title: How to Build an App With Claude Code – Full Tutorial for Beginners
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial walks complete beginners through building a real application using Claude Code from scratch, covering setup, prompting strategies, and iterating on results. It is an ideal starting point if you have just installed Claude Code and want a guided, hands-on introduction.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: A focused 30-minute YouTube walkthrough designed to take developers from zero to productive with Claude Code, covering key commands, VS Code integration tips, and best practices for prompting. This is a great watch for anyone who wants a concise but comprehensive overview without committing to a multi-hour course.

tips_of_the_day: Set `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_ALTERNATE_SCREEN=1` in your shell profile if you prefer to scroll back through Claude Code output in your normal terminal history rather than using the fullscreen renderer.

Before running Claude Code in any shared or CI environment, always confirm you are on the latest release — security fixes like the recent symlink sandbox-escape patch (CVE-2026-39861) ship as regular version updates.

Try prototyping new feature ideas as a single self-contained HTML file first — Claude Code generates clean HTML rapidly, giving you a tangible, shareable result in seconds that you can iterate on before wiring up a full framework.
